title: scaevola × cerasifolia
intro: Scaevola × cerasifolia, also known as the blue fan flower or fan flower, is a hybrid flowering plant that belongs to the Goodenia family. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Australian Aboriginal medicine, where it has been used to treat a range of health issues. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to reduce pain, fever, and swelling, as well as promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, Scaevola × cerasifolia is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat skin conditions, respiratory issues, and other ailments.
